title: Add Quiet to PullOptions and PushOptions
body:
Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

Add Quiet to the PullOptions and PushOptions structures.
If set to true output will be in 'quiet' mode.  This
will primarily be used by callers such as OpenShift.

Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

comments.body: so for BuildOptions (when invoking BuildDockerfiles) you'd just expect us to set ReportWriter=nil?

the push api also needs this.

so push, pull, build.  all need an explicit quiet flag (or you can tell us to just always set the reportwriter to nil)
@bparees I'll go ahead and add to push and build too.
@bparees I've added to PushOptions, looks like the option is already in play for BuildOptions.  @nalind PTAL

thanks

i'm guessing this won't be the end of this... if you look at our 3.x docker-based experience, we had decent logs that showed we were pushing layers so the user had some feedback, but not as verbose as what you get from buildah.  We are probably going to have to find a compromise position in between "no buildah output" and "the current semi verbose buildah output" in the future.  But for now at least this gives us a clean way to enable/disable.

:pushpin: Commit 5f892fe has been approved by `rhatdan`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=rhatdan 5f892fec1280ed43e94259d33f0a84f29d63a76e -->
:zap: Test exempted: pull fully rebased and already tested.
